validation_issues.message: Unused import: `TYPE_CHECKING` is imported from `typing` (line 21) but never referenced anywhere in the file. Remove it to keep imports clean.

`split_csv_int_df()` accepts a `spark: SparkSession` parameter (line 102) that is never used inside the function body. The function operates entirely via the `df` argument and native F.* expressions. Either remove the parameter or document why it is reserved for future use.

SparkSession (lines 190-194) is missing recommended AQE configs: `spark.sql.adaptive.enabled`, `spark.sql.adaptive.coalescePartitions.enabled`, `spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled`. Not critical for this utility, but required by the style guide for all SparkSession builders.

SparkSession is missing `.config("spark.sql.decimalOperations.allowPrecisionLoss", "false")`. Not exploitable here (no decimal arithmetic), but required by the style guide checklist for every SparkSession builder.
`run_pipeline()` uses an untyped `params: dict` (line 149) instead of a typed `PipelineParams` dataclass as required by the style guide. This loses IDE autocompletion and type-safety for callers.

File is missing a module-level docstring. Per the style guide, every output file must open with a `"""PySpark conversion of ..."""` docstring (Source, Target, SQL construct mapping, Usage). The file currently starts with `from __future__ import annotations` followed only by a block comment.

Docstring for `split_csv_int_df()` states 'DataFrame with all original columns replaced by output_col' (line 127), but the implementation uses `withColumn` which KEEPS all original columns and adds/replaces only the named column. The wording is misleading.

Runtime dependency unresolved: writes to Delta table 'dbo.AWLT_Converter_RunLog' via saveAsTable() (line 138), but the table definition file was not found. If this table does not already exist in the Spark metastore, every log_step() call will raise an AnalysisException at runtime. No CREATE TABLE / DDL fallback is present in this file.

SparkSession built without recommended configs: missing spark.sql.decimalOperations.allowPrecisionLoss=false, spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true, spark.sql.adaptive.coalescePartitions.enabled=true, spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true. Required by the style guide for all SparkSession instantiations.

F (pyspark.sql.functions) is imported but never used within this file. The inline comment '# noqa: F401 (available for callers)' is misleading — Python modules do not re-export names simply by importing them unless __all__ is defined or callers do 'from module import F'. This is a dead import that may confuse maintainers.

log_step() always sets StartedUtc = datetime.now(timezone.utc) and, when complete=True, sets CompletedUtc = that same instant. This means CompletedUtc will always equal StartedUtc (zero duration) for completed steps. The SQL original used a separate SYSUTCDATETIME() call per column, so durations were at least microseconds apart. Consider capturing the two timestamps independently if elapsed time is ever queried.
.collect() found — may cause OOM on large datasets
.collect() found — may cause OOM on large datasets
.collect() found — may cause OOM on large datasets
.collect() found — may cause OOM on large datasets

orders_df is unpersisted at line 1120 but then passed to _step9a_build_payload() at line 1134. Spark will silently recompute the full orders lineage from scratch (scan + join + filter), causing a major unintended performance regression. Fix: move orders_df.unpersist() to AFTER _step9a_build_payload() completes.

ConverterLogger stub is instantiated with zero arguments (line 1065: ConverterLogger()), but the real RunLogger in usp_awlt_converter_log.py requires __init__(self, spark, run_id, proc_name). Swapping in the real import will immediately raise TypeError. The instantiation call and the import alias must both be updated together.

QC_PREDICATE_MAP is an empty dict (lines 776-781). Every QC rule evaluated in _step10_evaluate_qc() falls into the else branch (line 829: failed = False), silently marking all QC checks as passed regardless of actual data state. QC_FailCount_Severity2/3 will always be 0 in the final result.

Dead code at line 1143: `_step10_evaluate_qc(spark, facts_df if False else rfm_df)`. The condition `if False` is always False so rfm_df is always used, but facts_df (already unpersisted at line 1126) is referenced in the expression, making intent unclear and masking a potential bug if the condition is ever changed to True.

spark.table("dbo.AWLT_QC_Definition") at line 810 references an object with no converted output file per the dependency manifest. This will raise AnalysisException at runtime unless the table is pre-registered in the Spark catalog.

SparkSession in main() (lines 1229-1233) is built without AQE or decimalOperations configs. These are applied later via spark.conf.set() inside run_pipeline(). Any caller that obtains the SparkSession before run_pipeline() executes will run without decimal precision protection until run_pipeline() is reached.

requires_manual.description: Runtime dependency unresolved: writes to Delta table 'dbo.AWLT_Converter_RunLog' via saveAsTable() (line 138), but the table definition file was not found. If this table does not already exist in the Spark metastore, every log_step() call will raise an AnalysisException at runtime. No CREATE TABLE / DDL fallback is present in this file.
requires_manual.how_to_fix: Review the issue manually and apply the appropriate PySpark fix. Refer to the PySpark migration guide for deprecated APIs.

recommended_review.description: .collect() found — may cause OOM on large datasets
recommended_review.how_to_fix: Avoid `.collect()` on large DataFrames. For single values use `.first()[col_name]` or `.agg(F.max(...)).collect()[0][0]`. For small reference data use `.limit(n).collect()`.
